task_completion.description: Load the raw FITS image sequence into the image processing software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the image processing software (e.g., AstroImageJ) open with the sequence of 20 images loaded from ~/AstroImages/raw_sequence/.

task_completion.description: Align the image stack to compensate for telescope drift.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An alignment dialog box, alignment progress bar, or the resulting aligned image stack showing black padding/borders at the edges where the images have been shifted to match.

task_completion.description: Define a Region of Interest (ROI) that excludes all zero-value padding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A selection rectangle or bounding box drawn on the image that strictly bounds the valid data region, completely excluding the black borders created by the alignment.

task_completion.description: Crop the image sequence to the defined ROI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image window showing the cropped stack with no black borders visible at the edges, indicating the crop operation was executed.

task_completion.description: Export the aligned and cropped stack as a FITS image sequence to the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Sequence' or export dialog pointing to ~/AstroImages/aligned_sequence/, or a file manager/terminal showing exactly 20 new FITS files in that directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface of an image processing tool (like AstroImageJ) to perform the alignment and cropping.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GUI menus, dialogs, and tools rather than just executing a terminal script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed an alignment operation rather than just cropping the raw images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of alignment settings dialogs or intermediate states showing shifted images with black borders before the crop is applied.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy the raw files to the output directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output files are generated through an export/save dialog in the software, and the final images visually lack the drift and black borders.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input directory is ~/AstroImages/raw_sequence/ containing exactly 20 FITS files with native dimensions of 4096x4096. The output directory must be ~/AstroImages/aligned_sequence/ and contain exactly 20 FITS files. The alignment must achieve < 1.0 px residual shift. The final images must be cropped to completely remove all zero-value (black) padding introduced by the alignment process. The software used is likely AstroImageJ.
